title: Enlightened Ego
definition: The illusion of a separate self or ego that persists even in states of nondual awareness, hindering the complete realization of unity and interconnectedness.

difficulty_levels.beginner: Even though we may have glimpses of unity and interconnectedness, there is still a tendency to cling to our separate identity. This ego can be a barrier to fully realizing the nondual state.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: In states of nondual awareness, the illusion of a separate self or ego may persist, hindering the complete realization of unity and interconnectedness. The enlightened ego is a remnant of dualistic thinking that clings to individual identity even in the face of the transcendent.
difficulty_levels.expert: The enlightened ego refers to the illusion of a separate self or ego that persists even in states of nondual awareness. This ego is a residual manifestation of dualistic thinking, which can hinder the complete realization of unity and interconnectedness.

extra_fields.insight: Even in states of nondual awareness, the illusion of a separate self or ego can persist, hindering the complete realization of unity and interconnectedness.
